X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
delete_device_function.name: device-function

delete_object_type.description: LOCAL - To delete local objects. SHARED - To delete shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
delete_object_type.name: object-type

delete_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
delete_scope_id.name: scope-id

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

ArubaAirgroupPolicy_AirgroupPoliciesSche.description:
An Airgroup policy must have a service definition, whereas VLAN and roles are optional.
In case of roles, both 'allowed-roles' and 'disallowed-roles' cannot be
configured at the same time. One of them must be empty. A maximum of 20 roles can be configured.
In case of VLANs, both 'allowed-vlans' and 'disallowed-vlans' cannot be
configured at the same time. One of them must be empty. A maximum of 20 VLANs or VLAN ranges
can be configured.
This feature is applicable for AP.

policy.description: List of policies for each Airgroup service to be applied at different scope.

name.description: Name of the Airgroup service. This should refer to one of the pre-defined services or a custom-service that has been defined by user.

server-expiry-time.description:
Duration in minutes after which the records
of inactive servers are purged. This timer will be used for
all server records belonging to this service. Allowed range
is from 60 (1 hour) to 360 (6 hours). If this is not
configured, the records are purged as per TTL/max-age specified
in packets

name.description: Name of the Airgroup service. This should refer to one of the pre-defined services or a custom-service that has been defined by user.

server-expiry-time.description:

of inactive servers are purged. This timer will be used for
all server records belonging to this service. Allowed range
is from 60 (1 hour) to 360 (6 hours). If this is not
configured, the records are purged as per TTL/max-age specified
in packets

role-restrictions.description: Role restriction for this service at global or group scope

allowed-roles.description:
List of allowed user Roles for this service. This
 must be empty if any roles are configured in
 'disallowed-roles'

role.description: User role that needs to be disallowed

disallowed-roles.description:
List of disallowed user Roles for this service.
This must be empty if any roles are configured in 'allowed-roles'

role.description: User role that needs to be disallowed

role-restriction-mode.description: Airgroup service will be restricted for configured roles and device type

vlan-restrictions.description:
Configuration of allowed/disallowed VLANs applicable to this service.
Both allowed and disallowed VLANS cannot be configured at the same time.

items.description:
List of allowed VLAN ids or range of VLAN ids. This
list must be empty if 'disallowed-vlans' has vlans configured
items.x-typeDescription:
VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

items.description:
List of disallowed VLAN ids or range of VLAN ids. This
list must be empty if 'allowed-vlans' has vlans configured
items.x-typeDescription:
VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

ArubaAirgroupServers_AirgroupServersSche.description:
Hosts that can act as Airgroup servers. The server can be restricted to specific role(s) and AP(s).
In case of roles, both 'allowed-roles' and 'disallowed-roles' cannot be configured at the same time.
One of them must be empty.  Maximum 20 roles can be configured.
In case of APs, a maximum of 50 APs can be configured

mac-address.description: MAC address of the Airgroup server

network-visibility.description:
Configuration to restrict visbility of Airgroup server to
specified AP or APs. To restrict to specific
APs, 'access-points' must be configured.

access-points.description:
List of APs which will process this server's
advertisements.

host-name.description:
Device Name. This field should be omitted for POST
operation and instead use 'serial-number' to identify the AP

role-restrictions.description: Configuration of Client Role restrictions applicable to this server

allowed-roles.description:

must be empty if any roles are configured in
'disallowed-roles'

This must be empty if any roles are configured in 'allowed-roles'

ArubaAirgroupServiceDefinition_AirgroupS.description:
A Airgroup service definition is a group of service ID(s), which requires
at least one custom service ID. A service ID can be of type mDNS or SSDP.
A maximum of 100 service IDs are allowed across all the services.
A maximum of 16 services are allowed and in each service 32 service IDs are allowed.
There are pre-defined services with default service IDs. These are read-only and
can be enabled or disabled. This feature is applicable for AP

service.description: Definition of Airgroup services that can have user specified protocol service-ids.

service-ids.description:
List of Service-IDs found in protocol packets that can be
used to identify this service.

enable.description:
Enable service-id as part of the parent standard service.
A standard service-id can be disabled if it needs to be
added in a custom service

service-id.description: Define MDNS or SSDP service ID

oneOf.x-typeDescription: Service id of type MDNS TCP
Service id of type MDNS UDP
Service id of type SSDP device
Service id of type SSDP service

added in a custom service

added in a custom service

ArubaAirgroupSystem_AirgroupSystemSchema.description:
AirGroup is a unique service that uses zero-configuration networking
 and allows devices to communicate over complex access-network topologies.
 AirGroup supports Bonjour and DLNA services on supported devices.
 This feature is applicable for AP

protocol.description: This option is used to enable only specific protocol for Airgroup feature, by default both MDNS and SSDP is supported

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: Airgroup supported for both MDNS and DLNA service
x-enumDescriptions.DLNA_ONLY: Airgroup supported only for DLNA service
x-enumDescriptions.MDNS_ONLY: Airgroup supported only for MDNS service

ArubaAirgroupSystem_AirgroupSystemSchema.description:

x-enumDescriptions.MDNS_ONLY: Airgroup supported only for MDNS service

cppm.description: Airgroup ClearPass Policy Manager Configuration Container

enforce-registration.description: When enabled, only devices registered with ClearPass Policy Manager will be discovered by Bonjour devices

query-interval.description: ClearPass Policy Manager query interval information for AirGroup policy.

server.description: Airgroup ClearPass Policy Manager server configuration

name.description: ClearPass Policy Manager server information for AirGroup policy.

guest-multicast.description: When enabled, Bonjour devices are visible only in guest VLAN and AirGroup will not discover or enforce policies in guest VLAN.

multi-swarm.description: When enabled, the Instant AP shares the mDNS/SSDP database information with the other clusters.

ArubaLocation_AleConfig.description: Top-level grouping for Analytics and Location Engine (ALE).

interval.description:
An interval at which the Virtual Controller can report
the AP and client details to the ALE server.
Range is from 6 seconds to 60 seconds, 30 by default.

server.description: Specify the FQDN (domain name) or IP address of the ALE server.

ArubaLocation_AleConfigGet.description: Top-level grouping for Analytics and Location Engine (ALE).

interval.description:
An interval at which the Virtual Controller can report
the AP and client details to the ALE server.
Range is from 6 seconds to 60 seconds, 30 by default.

server.description: Specify the FQDN (domain name) or IP address of the ALE server.

ArubaLocation_LocationSchemaGET.description:
Location configuration. It supports three methods for locating:
1, Received Signal Strength Indication (RSSI): Calculate the pathloss of signal to locate a device.
2, Analytics and Location Engine (ALE): The ALE is designed to gather client information from
   the network, process it, and share it through a standard API. The client information
   gathered by ALE can be used for business purposes by analyzing a client’s Internet behavior
   such as shopping preferences.
   ALE includes a location engine that calculates the associated and unassociated device
   location every 30 seconds by default.
3, Real Time Location System (RTLS): AP supports the real-time tracking of devices
   when integrated with the Airwave server or a third-party RTLS server such as
   Aeroscout RTLS server. With the help of the RTLS, the devices can be monitored in
   real time or through history.

This feature is only applicable for AP.

profile.description:
Location configuration. It supports three methods for locating:
1, Received Signal Strength Indication (RSSI): Calculate the pathloss of signal to locate a device.
2, Analytics and Location Engine (ALE): The ALE is designed to gather client information from
   the network, process it, and share it through a standard API. The client information
   gathered by ALE can be used for business purposes by analyzing a client’s Internet behavior
   such as shopping preferences.
   ALE includes a location engine that calculates the associated and unassociated device
   location every 30 seconds by default.
3, Real Time Location System (RTLS): AP supports the real-time tracking of devices
   when integrated with the Airwave server or a third-party RTLS server such as
   Aeroscout RTLS server. With the help of the RTLS, the devices can be monitored in
   real time or through history.

This feature is only applicable for AP.

rssi-report.description: Received Signal Strength Indication (RSSI) report parameters.

interval.description:
Time interval at which the AP sends RSSI to Central.
Range is from 5 seconds to 600 seconds, 60 by default.

level.description:
Signal strength, RSSI over this will be reported to Central, 0 means all data.
Range is from 0 to 100 in dBm, 0 by default.

type.description:
Set which data will be reported to Central.
Two options: UNASSOCIATED_CLIENTS_ONLY and UNASSOCIATED_AND_ASSOCIATED_CLIENTS.

x-enumDescriptions.UNASSOCIATED_AND_ASSOCIATED_CLIENTS: Both associated and unassociated clients will be reported.
x-enumDescriptions.UNASSOCIATED_CLIENTS_ONLY: Only unassociated clients will be reported.

type.x-typeDescription:
It defines different types of client, that will be involved in
RSSI (Received Signal Strength Indicator) report.

type-alias.description:
The user may apply aliases for device specific rssi-reporting when no rssi-report/type is configured, allowing
the system to define rssi-report/type parameters as needed on a scope specific basis.

rssi-report.description: Received Signal Strength Indication (RSSI) report parameters.

interval.description:
Time interval at which the AP sends RSSI to Central.
Range is from 5 seconds to 600 seconds, 60 by default.

level.description:
Signal strength, RSSI over this will be reported to Central, 0 means all data.
Range is from 0 to 100 in dBm, 0 by default.

type.description:
Set which data will be reported to Central.
Two options: UNASSOCIATED_CLIENTS_ONLY and UNASSOCIATED_AND_ASSOCIATED_CLIENTS.

x-enumDescriptions.UNASSOCIATED_AND_ASSOCIATED_CLIENTS: Both associated and unassociated clients will be reported.
x-enumDescriptions.UNASSOCIATED_CLIENTS_ONLY: Only unassociated clients will be reported.

type.x-typeDescription:
It defines different types of client, that will be involved in
RSSI (Received Signal Strength Indicator) report.

type-alias.description:

the system to define rssi-report/type parameters as needed on a scope specific basis.

rtls.description: Real Time Location System (RTLS) parameters on AP.

address.description:
IP address or domain name of the Aeroscout RTLS server to which the location reports are sent.
Support up to 255 characters.

port.description: Port number of the Aeroscout RTLS server to which the location reports are sent
port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

unassoc-sta.description:
Whether include the client stations not associated to any AP
when mobile unit reports are sent to the Aeroscout RTLS server.
Disabled by default.

address.description:
IP address or domain of the AirWave RTLS server.
Support up to 255 characters.

disable-server-compatibility.description:
Make Airwave RTLS report is incompatible with Aeroscout RTLS report.
False by default.

frequency.description:
Interval at which packets are sent to the RTLS server in seconds.
Range is from 0 second to 65535 seconds.

key.description: Key for service authorization, up to 255 characters.

port.description: Port number of the AirWave RTLS server.
port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

unassoc-sta.description:
Whether include the client stations not associated to any AP
when mobile unit reports are sent to the Aeroscout RTLS server.
False by default.

interval.description:
Interval at which the AP sends data on RTLS tags to Central.
Range is from 5 seconds to 600 seconds, 60 by default.

tag.description:
Enable RTLS tags to identify the location of assets.
Disable by default.

   real time or through history.

This feature is only applicable for AP.

get.description:
Location configuration. It supports three methods for locating:
1, Received Signal Strength Indication (RSSI): Calculate the pathloss of signal to locate a device.
2, Analytics and Location Engine (ALE): The ALE is designed to gather client information from
   the network, process it, and share it through a standard API. The client information
   gathered by ALE can be used for business purposes by analyzing a client’s Internet behavior
   such as shopping preferences.
   ALE includes a location engine that calculates the associated and unassociated device
   location every 30 seconds by default.
3, Real Time Location System (RTLS): AP supports the real-time tracking of devices
   when integrated with the Airwave server or a third-party RTLS server such as
   Aeroscout RTLS server. With the help of the RTLS, the devices can be monitored in
   real time or through history.

This feature is only applicable for AP.
